title: Brent, You Are Still #1
intro: Brent Faiyaz released 'Icon' on Valentine's Day and it is STILL in my rotation. Off first listen I loved nearly every track on this album. Since this album is only 10 songs and 1 is literally white noise, every second on this project matters. Brent, who is known for his toxic behavior in relationships has seemed to turn a new leaf and is now writing 'sing in the rain to her' ballads. He legitimately went slow-motion, walk-on-the-beach-in-the-rain for the 'wrong faces' music video. Four months later, Icon still has me in a chokehold, shoutout to Brent for this.

songs.title: have to.
songs.review: One of the lead singles from this project, and it absolutely set the tone. This is a serenade-worthy song, so start singing to your girl when the pre-chorus hits! I'm not sure if it's just my ears, but I couldn't quite understand what he was saying in the chorus. After reading the lyrics, I realized how ‘love song’ and dramatic it is, which only adds to its sentiment.

lines.text: If I have to, I'll drive this ship myself (All night)
If I have to, you can't be by yourself (Alone)
If you don't have to, I'll do what I have to

lyrics.caption: I think this the yearning y'all been looking for

lines.text: Uh, yeah, I'll go so far as to put myself inside my
Goyard if I have to (Would you believe it?)
I'm in love, you in love (Help), I'm above, you my drug

lyrics.caption: Love the callback to shipping himself to her if he needs to. More specifically a Goyard bag

songs.title: four seasons.
songs.review: This was instantly favorite song on first listen. I've seen some hot & cold behavior before, so I can tell Brent really speaking from the mind here.

songs.title: vanilla sky.
songs.review: The more I listen to Brent, the more alike we are. Maybe it's a September thing? This one was hitting hard some of my favorite lyrics on the entire project. The vocal runs towards then end are chef's kiss.

lines.text: Is this a safe space for me? (Mm)
To show this part of me? (That nobody sees)
Is this a safe space for me? (Safe space)

songs.title: wrong faces.
songs.review: This is the nastiest beat on the whole album. Thank you to each producer here, great work all around.

songs.title: butterflies.
songs.review: It doesn't sound bad just boring. All the lyrics are very vague and give off the vibe of someone who is writing a love song for the first time. But definitely not terrible, if I am listening through I can let it rock.
